title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs cloudfront origins, oac, sse-kms, and private delivery from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review S3 Origin Website Boundary
steps.instruction:

content.text: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 2439 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Oai Oac Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 2630 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Oai Oac Migration.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
